elements.sent-2411-001: We completely classify Hamiltonian stationary Lagrangian surfaces with harmonic mean curvature and constant curvature in complex space forms.
elements.sent-2411-002: Let $\tilde M^n$ be a complex $n$-dimensional K\"ahler manifold with the complex structure $J$ and the K\"ahler metric $\<\,, \>$.
elements.sent-2411-003: An $n$-dimensional submanifold $M$ of $\tilde M^n$ is called Lagrangian if $\<X, JY\>=0$ for all tangent vector fields $X$ and $Y$ of $M$.
elements.sent-2411-004: A normal vector field $\xi$ of a Lagrangian submanifold $M$ is called a {Hamiltonian variation if $\xi=J\nabla f$ for some compactly supported function $f$ on $M$, where $\nabla$ is the gradient on $M$.}
elements.sent-2411-005: A Lagrangian submanifold is said to be {Hamiltonian stationary if it is a critical point of the volume functional for all deformations with Hamiltonian variation vector fields.}
elements.sent-2411-006: A Lagrangian submanifold $M$ in $\tilde M^n$ is Hamiltonian stationary if and only if its mean curvature vector $H$ satisfies \be {\rm div (JH)=0\ee on $M$ (cf.}
elements.sent-2411-007: This implies that any Lagrangian submanifold with parallel mean curvature is Hamiltonian stationary.
elements.sent-2411-008: It is a fundamental and interesting problem to construct and classify Hamiltonian stationary Lagrangian submanifolds with non-parallel mean curvature in a specific K\"ahler manifold.
elements.sent-2411-009: A Hamiltonian stationary Lagrangian surface in a complex space form has constant mean curvature if and only if its mean curvature vector is parallel.
elements.sent-2411-010: Motivated by this fact, we investigate the case where the mean curvature is a non-constant harmonic function.
elements.sent-2411-011: In this paper, we completely classify Hamiltonian stationary Lagrangian surfaces with non-constant harmonic mean curvature and constant curvature in complex space forms.
elements.sent-2411-012: Let $\tilde M^n(4\epsilon)$ be a complete and simply connected complex space form of complex dimension $n$ and constant holomorphic sectional curvature $4\epsilon$, that is, $\tilde M^n(4\epsilon)$ is the complex Euclidean space $\mathbb{C ^n$, the complex projective space $\mathbb{C P^n(4\epsilon)$ or the complex hyperbolic space $\mathbb{C H^n(4\epsilon)$ according as $\epsilon=0$, $\epsilon>0$ or $\epsilon<0$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-013: Let $M$ be a Lagrangian submanifold of $\tilde M^n(4\epsilon)$.
elements.sent-2411-014: We denote the Levi-Civita connections on $M^n$ and $\tilde M^n(4\epsilon)$ by $\nabla$ and $\tilde\nabla$, respectively.

elements.sent-2411-075: In this paper, we deal with Hamiltonian stationary Lagrangian surfaces in complex space forms whose mean curvature is a harmonic function.
elements.sent-2411-076: We prove that if the mean curvature is non-zero constant, then the second fundamental form is parallel.
elements.sent-2411-077: In the non-constant mean curvature case, under the condition of constant Gaussian curvature, we give an explicit description of all such surfaces.
elements.sent-2411-078: In this paper, we deal with Hamiltonian stationary Lagrangian surfaces in complex space forms.
elements.sent-2411-079: First, we prove that if the mean curvature is non-zero constant, then the second fundamental form is parallel (Proposition \ref{pro ).}
elements.sent-2411-080: Next, we investigate the case where the mean curvature is a non-constant harmonic function.
elements.sent-2411-081: Our purpose is to give an explicit description of all such surfaces under the condition of constant Gaussian curvature (Theorem \ref{main ).}
elements.sent-2411-082: Denote by $K$ the Gaussian curvature of $M$.

elements.sent-2411-084: Then the equation (\ref{Gauss ) of Gauss and (\ref{sf ) yield that \be \delta=2b^2-ac+2c^2.. \ee.}}

elements.sent-2411-091: The map $\Pi: H_1^5(-1)\rightarrow {\mathbb C H^2(-4)$ defined by $z\mapsto z\cdot {\mathbb C ^{*}$ is referred to as the Hopf fibration.}}
elements.sent-2411-092: Let $M$ be a Hamiltonian stationary Lagrangian surface in a $2$-dimensional complex space form $\tilde M^2(4\epsilon)$, where $\epsilon\in\{-1, 0, 1\}$.
elements.sent-2411-093: If $M$ has non-constant harmonic mean curvature and constant Gaussian curvature $K$, then $K=\epsilon=-1$ and $M$ is locally congruent to the image of $\Pi\circ \phi$, where $\Pi: H_1^5(-1)\rightarrow {\mathbb C H^2(-4)$ is the Hopf fibration and $\phi: M\rightarrow H_1^5(-1)\subset {\mathbb C ^3_1$ is given by one of the following immersions$:$ $(1)$ \begin{equation}\phi (x, y)=\biggl(m e^{y}+\dfrac{e^{-y}+2im^2xe^{y}}{2m}, m e^{ix+y}, \dfrac{e^{-y}+2im^2xe^{y}}{2m}\biggr);\end{equation} $(2)$ \be \phi (x,y)=\biggl(1-\dfrac{i(1+m^2) {m^2x+y , \dfrac{m\sqrt{1+m^2 e^{ix {m^2x+y , \dfrac{\sqrt{1+m^2 e^{iy {m^2x+y}\biggr),\nonumber \ee where $m$ is a positive real number.}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-094: Differentiating (\ref{G1 ) with respect to $u$ and $v$, we obtain \begin{equation}b_u=\frac{cA+(a-4c)c_u}{4b}, \label{bu} b_v=\frac{cB+(a-4c)c_v}{4b}.\label{bv}\end{equation} Substituting (\ref{bu ) and (\ref{bv ) into (\ref{au ) and (\ref{av ) yields \begin{equation}4abc_u-a(a-4c)c_v=4b(3c-a)A-(12b^2-ac)B, \label{cu} a(a-4c)c_u+4abc_v=(12b^2-ac)A+12bcB. \label{cv}\end{equation} Solving (\ref{cu ) and (\ref{cv ) for $c_u$ and $c_v$ and using (\ref{G1 ), we have \begin{equation}c_u=\frac{Af_1+Bf_2}{a(a^2+8\delta)},\label{Cu} c_v=\frac{Af_3+Bf_4}{a(a^2+8\delta)},\label{Cv}\end{equation} where \begin{equation}f_1=-4ab^2-a^2c+4ac^2, f_2=-48b^3+16abc-48bc^2, f_3=4a^2b+48b^3-32abc+48bc^2, f_4=12ab^2-a^2c+4ac^2.\end{equation}.}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-095: Substituting (\ref{Cu ) and (\ref{Cv ) into (\ref{bu ) and (\ref{bv ) gives \begin{equation}b_u= \frac{Ag_1+Bg_2}{a(a^2+8\delta)},\label{Bu} b_v= \frac{Ag_3+Bg_4}{a(a^2+8\delta)},\label{Bv}\end{equation} where \begin{equation}g_1=-a^2 b + 8 a b c, g_2=-12ab^2+4a^2c+48b^2c -28ac^2+48c^3, g_3=a^3+12ab^2-12a^2c-48b^2c+44ac^2-48c^3, g_4=3 a^2 b - 8 a b c.\end{equation}.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-096: Differentiating (\ref{Cu ) and (\ref{Cv ) with respect to $v$ and $u$ respectively, we have \begin{equation}c_{uv}=\frac{(Af_1+Bf_2)_va(a^2+8\delta)-(Af_1+Bf_2)(3a^2+8\delta)B}{a^2(a^2+8\delta)^2}, \label{Cuv} c_{vu}=\frac{(Af_3+Bf_4)_ua(a^2+8\delta)-(Af_3+Bf_4)(3a^2+8\delta)A}{a^2(a^2+8\delta)^2}. \label{Cvu}\end{equation} By a straightforward but tedious computation, using (\ref{Cu )-(\ref{Bv ) and substituting (\ref{G1 ) into the numerators of (\ref{Cuv ) and (\ref{Cvu ), we get \begin{equation}c_{uv}=\frac{A^2h_1+ABh_2+B^2h_3}{a^2(a^2+8\delta)^2}, \label{cuv} c_{vu}=\frac{A^2h_4+ABh_5+B^2h_6}{a^2(a^2+8\delta)^2}, \label{cvu}\end{equation} where \begin{equation}h_1= -12 a^4 b - 144 a^2 b^3 + 160 a^3 b c + 768 a b^3 c - 656 a^2 b c^2 + 768 a b c^3, h_2= -108 a^3 b^2 - 960 a b^4 + 18 a^4 c + 1168 a^2 b^2 c + 2304 b^4 c - 252 a^3 c^2 -3840 a b^2 c^2 + 1296 a^2 c^3 + 4608 b^2 c^3 - 2880 a c^4 + 2304 c^5, h_3= -96 a^2 b^3 + 768 b^5 - 384 a b^3 c + 32 a^2 b c^2 + 1536 b^3 c^2 - 384 a b c^3 + 768 b c^4, h_4= -8 a^4 b - 96 a^2 b^3 - 768 b^5 + 128 a^3 b c + 1152 a b^3 c - 608 a^2 b c^2 -1536 b^3 c^2 + 1152 a b c^3 - 768 b c^4, h_5= -92 a^3 b^2 - 192 a b^4 + 18 a^4 c + 784 a^2 b^2 c + 2304 b^4 c - 252 a^3 c^2 -3072 a b^2 c^2 + 1296 a^2 c^3 + 4608 b^2 c^3 - 2880 a c^4 + 2304 c^5, h_6= -240 a^2 b^3 + 80 a^3 b c + 768 a b^3 c - 496 a^2 b c^2 + 768 a b c^3.\end{equation}.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-097: Since $c_{uv -c_{vu}$=0 holds, from (\ref{cuv ), (\ref{cvu ), it follows that \be 4b(A^2k_1+ABk_2+B^2k_3)=0, \ee where \begin{equation}k_1= -a^4 - 12 a^2 b^2 + 192 b^4 + 8 a^3 c - 96 a b^2 c - 12 a^2 c^2 + 384 b^2 c^2 - 96 a c^3 + 192 c^4, k_2= -4 a^3 b - 192 a b^3+ 96 a^2 b c - 192 a b c^2, k_3= 36 a^2 b^2 + 192 b^4 - 20 a^3 c - 288 a b^2 c + 132 a^2 c^2 + 384 b^2 c^2 - 288 a c^3 + 192 c^4.\end{equation}.}}}
elements.sent-2411-099: Combining (\ref{G1 ) and (\ref{R1 ), we find that (\ref{R1 ) can be simplified to \be B^9 \delta^3 (a^2 + 8 \delta)^7 (a^2 + 24 \delta)^2 (5 a^6 + 60 a^4 \delta + 180 a^2 \delta^2 + 288 \delta^3), \ee which must vanish on $\mathcal{V}$.}}}
